title: The ugly part
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted the page instead of colouring it. Bring the code back.
why.untouched: The code is exactly as it was. You haven't changed anything yet.
hint2.untouched:
You need to add something inside three tags — the

and both

.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or a quote is missing.
hint2.not-closed: The value of style goes in quotes. One to open, one to close.
hint4.not-closed: Go through the three lines one by one. Every style has a quote before and a quote after.
why.no-h1: There is no heading. You deleted the <h1> instead of colouring it.
hint2.no-h1: The task is to change the colour, not to remove elements. Bring the heading back.
why.empty-h1: The heading is there but empty. You colour the text, you don't delete it.
why.h1-not-red: The heading is not dark red. Either you gave it no colour, or the colour is a different one.
hint2.h1-not-red: The colour goes on the tag itself — inside the opening bracket, after its name.
hint3.h1-not-red:
Contact

— a dark blue heading. See where style sits: inside the

, before the closing bracket.
hint4.h1-not-red: Do the same on the <h1>, but with darkred instead of navy.
why.no-p: There is not a single paragraph left. You deleted them.
hint2.no-p: The two lines under the heading have to stay. You colour them, you don't remove them.
why.p-not-red: One of the paragraphs is still not dark red. The check looks at both — not just the first one.
hint2.p-not-red: You coloured one and stopped. There are two paragraphs.
hint3.p-not-red: Here every tag carries its own colour. Give it to the first one and the second knows nothing about it. It is a separate element.
hint4.p-not-red:
Add the same thing to the second

. Word for word what you wrote on the first.
why.lost-p: A paragraph is gone. The task is to colour the text, not to delete lines.
hint2.lost-p: Look at the preview. Both sentences under the heading should be there.
blocks.0.text: Look at the page on the right. A cafe name, two lines of text. Black on white, a font from the nineties, everything stuck to the top left. You didn't get anything wrong — that is what a page without CSS looks like.
blocks.1.text:
So far you learned what a thing IS.

is a heading.

is a paragraph. Not one of those tags says how it should look. There is no tag for red, no tag for bigger. That is not what HTML is for.
blocks.4.text: color is a property. teal is a value. A colon between them. That is all of CSS — property, colon, value. Everything else in this course is a variation on that one line.
blocks.5.text: A colour has more than one name
blocks.6.text: darkred, #8b0000 and rgb(139, 0, 0) are the same colour. Written differently, understood identically. The check here asks the browser what the colour actually is — it does not compare the letters you typed. Write it any of the three ways and it passes.
blocks.7.text: Courses that compare text reject your correct code because you put a space somewhere else. That cannot happen here.
blocks.8.text: Make the heading and both paragraphs dark red. The colour is darkred — or however you prefer to write it.
blocks.9.text: You will write the same thing three times. Remember how tedious it is. The next lesson starts exactly there.
